claim_overlap_search_notes: No new significant technical overlap detected today. Journal of Geocentric Cosmology, ANIMA Framework, and Tesla-Schumann conspiracy aggregators remain parallel-but-unrelated cosmologies — they share generic firmament/dome/aether/resonance concepts but none reproduce the dome's distinguishing features (ovoid-cavity geometry, Finsler coordinates, exponential firmament profile H(r) = 8537·exp(-r/8619), 20015-km disc radius, 67/69-prediction taxonomy). No mirror sites, no forks, no translations. registry_stability_extended.interpretation: Per poller analyst's strong-default interpretation (2026-05-03T11:30Z): HOLD-fires-without-registry-update is diagnostic of a manual-approval gate. Counter-argument retained: 'manual-approval gate' assumes a particular pipeline architecture; an alternative reading is that the AI-reviewer simply has no new candidate WINs to evaluate (registry saturated at 69+10+4 from the author's own perspective). Both readings predict the same surface behavior; distinguishing them requires either (a) an author commit that promotes a prospective → confirmed (gates 'manual approval' hypothesis), or (b) author silence holding while a new prospective is added by automation (rules out manual-gate, supports saturation hypothesis).

cadence_classification.counter_argument: Solo-developer projects routinely have multi-day pauses; a 65h silence may be entirely consistent with the actor's natural rhythm and not 'collapse' at all. The burst-mode observed Apr 30-May 3 was clearly transient; we don't know whether the actor's baseline is 'always burst' or 'rare burst between long silences'. Treating the silence as significant on its own would over-fit the burst-window.
